articles.headline: IndiGo Reports First Annual Loss in Three Years Amid Rupee Fall
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation, operator of IndiGo, reported a loss of ₹2,393.6 crore for the fiscal year ended March 2026, its first since FY23. The loss is attributed to an 11% depreciation of the rupee against the dollar, which increased leased liabilities. While revenue from operations rose 5% to ₹84,962 crore, yield fell 3%, indicating the airline flew more seats at lower fares.

articles.headline: Direct Retail Equity Ownership in NSE Firms Hits Five Year Low
articles.summary: Individual direct ownership of NSE-listed companies fell to 9.1% in Q4 FY26, the lowest level since FY21. The decline is attributed to market volatility, tepid earnings growth, and a shift toward mutual funds and ETFs. Conversely, domestic mutual fund ownership reached a record 11.4%. Combined, individuals and funds held 18.7% of the market, exceeding FPI holdings for six quarters.

articles.headline: Blue Origin New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Florida Launchpad Test
articles.summary: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin suffered a setback as its New Glenn rocket exploded during a test at a Florida launchpad. No personnel were injured, and no satellites were on board. The rocket is critical for Amazon’s Kuiper satellite network and NASA's Artemis program. The failure comes as the firm tries to compete with SpaceX, with New Glenn already years behind its original schedule.

articles.headline: Investors Scrutinize Token Costs as AI Startups Face Profitability Pressure
articles.summary: Investors are increasingly questioning AI startups on unit economics and token costs. Excessive usage of expensive models like OpenAI or Anthropic for minor tasks is seen as a lack of discipline. Indian AI startups raised $721 million in early 2026. Founders are now optimizing costs by using cheaper open-source models for routine processing and reserving premium models for production.

articles.headline: Corporate America Rations AI Usage as Computing Expenses Skyrocket
articles.summary: Large firms including Uber, Meta, and Microsoft are beginning to limit employee access to expensive AI tools to manage costs. Some companies reported hitting annual AI budgets within three months. Efforts include steering workers toward cheaper homegrown tools and implementing usage-based pricing. The shift highlights a move from experimental spending to requiring tangible productivity gains.

articles.headline: Drug Inspectors to Audit Spas and Salons Over Unauthorized Medical Procedures
articles.summary: The Drug Controller General of India (DCGI) has directed state inspectors to conduct surprise audits of spas and salons. The move targets unauthorized medical procedures like glutathione infusions, often sold as beauty treatments. These procedures violate the Cosmetics Rules, 2020. Regulators aim to enforce quality standards in a $20 billion industry where non-sterile procedures pose health risks.

articles.headline: Indian Super League Faces Existential Crisis Following East Bengal Victory
articles.summary: Despite East Bengal FC winning the 2025-26 title, the ISL faces instability. The AIFF failed to secure a long-term commercial partner after FSDL's contract ended. The recent season was truncated to 13 games with many players facing salary cuts. Nearly 150 players will be out of contract by June 1. Clubs are demanding a more sustainable league model to avoid financial collapse.

articles.headline: Debt Conversion Strategy Lifts Reliance Retail FY26 Profits by Three Thousand Crore
articles.summary: Reliance Retail Ltd (RRL) reported a profit of ₹13,476 crore in FY26, up ₹3,503 crore year-on-year. The growth was largely aided by converting ₹40,000 crore of interest-bearing inter-company loans into zero-coupon optionally fully convertible debentures (OFCDs). This move reduced finance costs from ₹4,725 crore to ₹1,510 crore, effectively shifting profit from parent RRVL to the subsidiary.

articles.headline: Sebi Reverses Mandatory MF Nomination Rules After Operational Challenges
articles.summary: Sebi has rolled back parts of its January 2025 framework on nominations for demat accounts and mutual fund folios. Mandatory disclosure is now restricted to the nominee's name and relationship, while other details remain optional. The regulator dropped the plan to increase the nominee limit to 10, keeping it at three. The move follows feedback that the previous rules hampered investor onboarding.

articles.headline: Asian Paints Beats Estimates With Eighteen Percent Profit Growth
articles.summary: Asian Paints reported an 18% rise in profit to ₹4,325.35 crore for FY26. Revenue from operations grew 5% to ₹35,583.54 crore, exceeding analyst estimates. Managing director Amit Syngle noted that while input costs remain high, the firm has only passed on partial costs to consumers via a 10.5-11% price hike. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia remain a concern for future demand and inflation.

articles.headline: Government Considers Social Security Schemes for Ten Million Gig Workers
articles.summary: India's labour ministry is consulting with fund managers to introduce social security schemes for gig workers. There are currently 10 million gig workers in India, a figure expected to rise to 25 million by 2030. The schemes would target individuals engaged with food delivery and ride-hailing platforms. This initiative aims to provide a safety net for a rapidly growing informal workforce.

articles.headline: SAIC to Sell Extra Stake in MG Motor Venture to JSW
articles.summary: China’s SAIC Motor is set to sell an additional 10% stake in JSW MG Motor to its local partner JSW. This transaction will make JSW the largest shareholder in the unit, reducing SAIC's stake to 39%. The deal follows the Indian government's increased scrutiny of Chinese investments. SAIC and JSW entered the venture to expand MG Motor’s presence in India's growing electric vehicle market.

articles.headline: Oil Companies Tasked With Building Thirty Day LPG Storage Buffer
articles.summary: The federal oil ministry has directed state-run fuel retailers to increase LPG storage capacity to meet 30 days of demand. Currently, storage levels are lower, and the move aims to ensure energy security. Joint Secretary Sujata Sharma also stated that India is working on increasing crude oil storage. The directive comes amid volatile global energy markets and supply chain concerns.

articles.headline: Sagarmala Finance to Issue India First Blue Bond for Coastal Projects
articles.summary: Sagarmala Finance Corp. plans to issue India's first 'blue bond' to raise funds for ocean and water-related infrastructure. The bonds target investors mandated to support environmental focuses. Funds will be used to develop maritime and coastal projects. Blue bonds are a relatively new financial instrument in India designed to support sustainable marine and fisheries economies.

articles.headline: Power Firms Quadruple Domestic Gas Purchases to Meet Summer Demand
articles.summary: Indian power generation companies increased natural gas purchases from domestic bourses by 350% between April and May. The surge to 4.5 trillion BTUs was driven by extreme summer heat. Firms are using gas-based power to meet high night-time demand when solar power is unavailable. The Indian Gas Exchange reported this significant spike compared to the same period in 2025.

articles.headline: Cockroach Janta Party Website Blocked on Major Corporate Intranets
articles.summary: Employees at audit firms like PwC, KPMG, and Deloitte reported being unable to access the 'Cockroach Janta Party' (CJP) satirical website through office networks. Mainstream political party sites remain accessible. CJP gained millions of followers after satirical remarks on a Supreme Court judge. While companies cite security, legal experts suggest blocking URLs may violate freedom of expression.

articles.headline: Anderson Diagnostics Seeks Two Hundred Fifty Crore Rupee Investment Round
articles.summary: Chennai-based Anderson Diagnostics and Labs is looking to raise ₹250 crore through a mix of primary and secondary transactions. Tata Capital Healthcare Fund, which holds a minority stake, may partially exit. The funds will be used to expand the firm's processing network in South India. The firm reported FY25 revenue of ₹203.3 crore and specializes in oncology and clinical genetics.

articles.headline: Satwik Chirag Badminton Duo Remains Resilient Despite Lack of Recent Titles
articles.summary: India’s leading doubles pair, Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty, are focused on recovery and upcoming major events like the World Championships in Delhi. Despite not winning a title in two years, they maintain a world ranking of four and recently won a Thomas Cup bronze. Rankireddy expressed frustration over the lack of public recognition for non-gold sporting achievements.

articles.headline: Indian Artists to Feature in Major State Hermitage Museum Exhibition
articles.summary: Eleven Indian artists, including Afrah Shafiq and V. Ramesh, will showcase works at 'Sediments of Becoming' at the State Hermitage Museum in St. Petersburg. The exhibition, running June to October, explores cultural borders and identity. It is one of the first dedicated Indian contemporary art shows in the museum's 260-year history and includes responses to the museum's own collections.

articles.headline: Smartphone Boom Drives Gaming Growth in Small Town India
articles.summary: India is forecast to have 724 million gamers by 2029, with mobile accounting for nearly 80% of the market. Wireless internet expansion has democratized gaming, allowing players in tier-2 and tier-3 towns to access the same titles as those in metros. This trend is turning small-town gamers into a new 'tech elite,' with many leveraging early technical literacy into professional careers.

articles.headline: India Projected to Drive Twenty Percent of Global Pet Adoption
articles.summary: Satinder Singh of Royal Canin states that India will contribute significantly to the 85% global rise in pet adoption coming from emerging markets. The Indian pet care market, valued at $786.6 million in 2024, is attracting major players like Godrej and Nestle. Younger consumers are increasingly treating pets as family members, driving demand for specialized nutrition and healthcare products.

articles.headline: Gujarat Solidifies Status as India Unofficial Ice Cream Capital
articles.summary: Driven by long summers, strong dairy infrastructure, and prohibition, Gujarat has become India's leading ice cream market. Per capita consumption in the state is over 3 litres, compared to the national average of 1.6 litres. Major brands like Amul, Vadilal, and Hocco coexist with local parlours offering regional flavors like ginger, meetha paan, and guava chilli to a loyal customer base.

articles.headline: Data Centres Water Consumption Sparks Environmental and Resource Concerns
articles.summary: Nisha Susan highlights the environmental impact of data centres, noting they used 140 billion litres of water for cooling in 2023. As companies plan new facilities in cities like Hyderabad, which faces a massive daily water shortage, the 'scorched-earth' potential of these investments is questioned. The piece argues against the inevitability of resource-heavy AI and data infrastructure.

articles.headline: Nurturing Emotional Well Being Through Mono Tasking and Rest
articles.summary: Psychotherapist Sonali Gupta argues that physical sleep is insufficient for emotional recovery. She suggests that true rest involves 'emotional nourishment' through hobbies like painting or trekking and active 'mono-tasking.' Constant task-switching and digital scrolling are identified as major causes of chronic fatigue, and individuals are encouraged to make space for fun and play.

articles.headline: Trump nears final decision on 60-day Iran truce extension
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump announced he is making a 'final determination' on a preliminary deal to extend a ceasefire with Iran for 60 days. The deal involves discussions on Tehran’s nuclear programme and keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. While Trump expressed optimism, Iran’s semi-official Tasnim agency maintains that no final understanding has been reached between the two nations.

articles.headline: NFHS-6 data shows major gains in health cover and digital access
articles.summary: The National Family and Health Survey (NFHS-6) for 2023-24 reveals significant social progress in India. Health insurance coverage rose to 60.2% from 41% in 2019-21. Internet usage among women surged to 64.3% from 33.3%, while bank account ownership reached 89%. India's total fertility rate remains at 2, below the replacement level of 2.1, indicating a long-term population decline.

articles.headline: Institutional deliveries in India reach 90.6% as public facility births drop
articles.summary: Data from NFHS-6 shows institutional deliveries in India have risen to 90.6% from 88.6%. However, births in public facilities decreased from 61.9% to 58.6%. Safe childbirth progress continues with 91.3% of births attended by skilled personnel. Postnatal care within 48 hours improved to 82.8%. Experts attribute the shift to private facilities to empanelment under the AB-PMJAY scheme.

articles.headline: Education Ministry to audit CBSE on-screen marking system after glitches
articles.summary: The Union education ministry will conduct a full audit of CBSE’s on-screen marking (OSM) system following widespread concerns over evaluation glitches. An internal report from a January dry run had already flagged 36 technical and operational risks, including 'blind checking' and data loss. The audit will cover the entire process from tendering to execution to stabilize the re-evaluation portal.

articles.headline: NTA to use AI for 85% of exam translation work
articles.summary: Following a 'traumatic' NEET-UG 2026 cancellation, the National Testing Agency told the Supreme Court it will overhaul its architecture by using AI tools for at least 85% of translation work. The agency is also considering a transition to computer-based testing (CBT) and blockchain-backed security. The court directed the Centre to ensure measures are in place to prevent recurring paper leaks.

articles.headline: Storms and lightning kill 30 people across five Indian states
articles.summary: At least 30 people were killed by thunderstorms and lightning across India. Uttar Pradesh reported 11 deaths, including six workers killed when an under-construction bridge over the Betwa river in Hamirpur collapsed. West Bengal and Bihar each recorded seven deaths, while Madhya Pradesh saw three and Haryana two. The weather disruption caused widespread damage to houses and infrastructure.

articles.headline: India-US interim trade deal nearing completion with 1% left
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor stated that 99% of a bilateral interim trade deal with India has been finalized. An American negotiating team is set to visit New Delhi from June 1-4 to conclude the remaining details. The agreement aims to unlock economic prosperity and deepen collaboration in AI, pharmaceuticals, and critical minerals. Bilateral trade has grown from $20 billion to $220 billion.

articles.headline: Netanyahu highlights India's 'crazy love' for Israel amid global criticism
articles.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu described India as a 'huge power' where there is an 'absolutely crazy love' for Israel, contrasting it with delegitimization efforts elsewhere. Speaking in the Jordan Valley, he noted he has more followers from India than anywhere else. Netanyahu emphasized the importance of expanding alliances with powers like India during a leadership programme.

articles.headline: Indian Navy drops plan to indigenise non-officer ranks
articles.summary: The Indian Navy has quietly abandoned a proposal to replace colonial-era ranks for its non-officer cadre with Indian titles. The plan, which would have affected over 65,000 sailors, was dropped as the defence ministry believes such changes should be implemented uniformly across all three services. The navy had previously introduced a new ensign and discarded batons to shed British traditions.

articles.headline: Delhi identifies 27 hotspots for new multilevel parking infrastructure
articles.summary: Authorities have shortlisted 27 congestion hotspots in Delhi for new parking facilities, including sites near New Delhi metro station, Saket District Centre, and Old Delhi railway station. The project involves multiple agencies like MCD, DDA, and DMRC under a proposed unified parking management authority. The plan is currently in the preliminary feasibility and financial assessment stage.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court fines Google ₹30 lakh for trademark infringement
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ordered Google to pay ₹30 lakh to Hindware for allowing rivals to use the company's trademark as an advertising keyword. The court found that Google's AdWords service facilitates 'free-riding' on Hindware’s reputation. Google was stripped of 'safe harbour' protection under the IT Act as it was found to be actively facilitating the use of trademarks as inventory.

articles.headline: Congress leaders meet in Delhi to finalize Karnataka cabinet transition
articles.summary: Top Congress leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi, met with Siddaramaiah and DK Shivakumar in Delhi to discuss Karnataka's leadership transition. Parleys focused on cabinet composition, the number of deputy CMs, and a future role for Siddaramaiah, potentially as chairman of a guarantee schemes committee. A CLP meeting is scheduled for Saturday in Bengaluru to elect a new leader.

articles.headline: AAP wins 48% wards in Punjab civic body elections
articles.summary: The ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) secured 954 out of 1,977 wards in the Punjab civic body elections. Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann hailed the result as a rejection of 'politics of hatred.' The Congress won 393 wards, while the SAD and BJP took 192 and 172 respectively. AAP won corporations in Moga, Barnala, and Amritsar, but the BJP emerged as the largest party in Pathankot and Abohar.

articles.headline: Shubman Gill powers Gujarat Titans into IPL 2026 final
articles.summary: Shubman Gill scored a commanding 104 to lead Gujarat Titans to a seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier 2. GT successfully chased down a target of 215 at the PCA Stadium in Chandigarh. The win sets up a final clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday. RR's 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stood out in defeat with a blistering 47-ball 96.

articles.headline: Rishabh Pant steps down as LSG captain after poor season
articles.summary: Rishabh Pant has resigned as the captain of Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) following the team's last-place finish in IPL 2026. The franchise accepted his request to step down as part of a restructuring effort. Pant, the league's most expensive player at ₹27 crore, struggled with form, scoring 312 runs this season. Reports suggest he may enter the auction pool or seek a trade.

articles.headline: 14 dead in Pune hooch tragedy; eight people arrested
articles.summary: At least 14 people died and four remain in critical condition after consuming suspected spurious liquor in Maharashtra’s Pune and Pimpri Chinchwad. Initial investigations suggest methanol was used in the product. Police and the excise department have arrested eight people and identified a supply chain spanning multiple locations. The probe has been transferred to the CID.

articles.headline: Japan records sharpest population fall as birth rates continue to lag
articles.summary: Japan's latest census shows the sharpest population decline on record, with residents decreasing by 2 million over five years to 123 million. Births fell for the 10th straight year in 2025, with deaths now doubling births. The crisis is spreading nationwide, hitting northern prefectures hardest and leading to labor shortages. Projections suggest the population could fall to 87 million by 2070.

articles.headline: Six killed in coordinated assault over land dispute in Vijayapura
articles.summary: Six people, including five from the prominent Nirale family, were killed in Govindapura village, Karnataka. More than 10 assailants attacked the victims with sugarcane machetes and firearms after throwing chilli powder into their eyes. The violence stems from a 15-year dispute over 25 acres of land. Police have launched a search for the attackers and stationed personnel in the village.

articles.headline: Bengaluru man arrested for forging papers to claim BDA lands
articles.summary: Police arrested 45-year-old Shaloom Kumar for allegedly creating forged General Power of Attorney documents to claim Bengaluru Development Authority properties worth several crores. The fraud targeted sites in Arkavathi and OMBR Layouts using false documents under the name of the Greater Bengaluru Authority. A special investigation team is seeking more suspects in the wider fraud network.

articles.headline: Opposition parties in Telangana oppose creation of third power discom
articles.summary: The BRS and independent experts opposed the Telangana government's proposal to form a dedicated electricity distribution company (Rythu Discom) for farmers. During a public hearing, BRS leaders alleged the move is a step toward privatizing the power sector and abandoning the free electricity policy. The government claims the new entity will ensure better quality supply for agricultural needs.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu transfers senior IAS officers and district collectors
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government implemented a major administrative reshuffle, transferring several senior IAS officers and collectors. E Sundaravalli was moved to Public and Rehabilitation, while P Ponniah became Commissioner of Collegiate Education. Collectors in over 15 districts, including Madurai, Tiruchirappalli, and Vellore, were also reassigned in the state release issued on Friday.

articles.headline: TN Minister's surprise inspection at Tiruchendur temple exposes corruption
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Minister S Ramesh conducted a surprise visit to the Subramaniya Swamy Temple in Tiruchendur disguised as a devotee. During the check, a priest allegedly demanded ₹1,000 for a 'quick darshan' despite the official fee being ₹100. One priest was suspended, and four other staff members were removed from service. The minister warned of strict action against temple irregularities.

articles.headline: Waqf Board lawyer's death in Hyderabad was contract killing: Police
articles.summary: Hyderabad Police confirmed that the hit-and-run death of Waqf Board lawyer Khaja Moizuddin on May 23 was a 'meticulous contract killing.' Seven people, including Telangana Congress leader Mujahid Alam Khan and his father, were arrested for the conspiracy. The murder was linked to deep-seated legal disputes. The Congress party has suspended both leaders following their arrest.

articles.headline: CBI files first charge sheet against Anil Ambani-led Reliance Group
articles.summary: The CBI filed its first charge sheet in the Reliance Group probe, naming 16 accused including Reliance Communications Ltd and senior executives. The case involves the alleged misuse of ₹2,050 crore in credit facilities from SBI, Bank of Maharashtra, and Syndicate Bank. Charges include criminal conspiracy, cheating, and corruption. Supplementary charge sheets are expected soon.

articles.headline: State must compensate for every day of illegal detention: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the State is obligated to compensate individuals for every day of illegal custody, stating personal liberty cannot be subordinated to bureaucratic delay. The bench ordered the Rajasthan government to pay ₹11 lakh to a man detained for 24 days after a release order. The court held that the State must comply with judicial orders unless stayed by a superior court.

articles.headline: Pooja Singh sets new high jump senior national record
articles.summary: 19-year-old Pooja Singh shattered the senior national high jump record by clearing 1.93m at the Asian U20 Athletics Championships in Hong Kong. She surpassed Sahana Kumari's 14-year-old record of 1.92m. Pooja, who won gold at the event, also equalled the 2026 Commonwealth Games qualifying mark. Her performance marks her as a leading talent in Indian athletics.

articles.headline: Government Launches Shared Portal to Track Unclaimed Financial Assets
articles.summary: Financial Services Secretary M. Nagaraju launched a common portal to simplify the process for citizens to claim unclaimed deposits, insurance, shares, dividends, and mutual funds. The platform aims to improve accessibility and security by providing a single point of search across multiple financial institutions.

articles.headline: Vedanta Resources to Invest 20 Billion Dollars Following Strategic Restructuring
articles.summary: Vedanta founder Anil Agarwal announced a $20 billion investment plan over the next 2-3 years, targeting $50 billion in revenue. The group is undergoing a historic demerger into four core businesses to enhance transparency and independence, focusing on aluminum, zinc, and critical minerals recycling.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Sees Highest Single-Day Gain in Two Months
articles.summary: The Indian rupee rose by 0.73% to close at 95 per US dollar, its highest level since May 8. The surge was driven by falling crude oil prices and likely intervention by the RBI in the non-deliverable forward (NDF) market to meet high dollar demand and offset recent losses.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty Slump Over 1.4 Percent on MSCI Rebalancing
articles.summary: Domestic benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty fell significantly as foreign portfolio investors exited positions during MSCI index rebalancing. The Sensex dropped 1,092 points to close at 74,776, while Nifty shed 359 points. Heavyweights like HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries led the decline.

articles.headline: NFHS-6 Survey Highlights Growing Burden of Obesity and Diabetes
articles.summary: The National Family Health Survey-6 reveals a significant rise in obesity and diabetes across India. Obesity among women increased to 30.7%, while the proportion of men taking medication for diabetes rose to 20.9%. However, the survey noted improvements in health insurance coverage, child vaccination, and maternal care indicators.

articles.headline: CMR Green Technologies to Launch 630 Crore Rupee IPO
articles.summary: Metal recycling firm CMR Green Technologies will launch its ₹630 crore initial public offering on June 3. The price band is set at ₹182-192 per share, valuing the company at ₹4,206 crore. The offer consists entirely of an offer for sale (OFS) by existing promoters.

articles.headline: Coal India OFS Retail Segment Subscribed 1.5 Times on Friday
articles.summary: The retail portion of the Coal India Offer for Sale (OFS) saw strong demand, receiving bids for 1.84 crore shares against the 1.23 crore offered. The government expects to raise over ₹5,000 crore through the stake sale, which was priced at a floor price of ₹412 per share.

articles.headline: Kotak Mahindra AMC Launches Nifty Alpha Low-Volatility 30 Index Fund
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Asset Management Company has introduced an open-ended scheme tracking the Nifty Alpha Low-Volatility 30 Index. The fund selects stocks based on a combination of high alpha and low volatility factors. The New Fund Offer (NFO) is open from May 29 to June 12.

articles.headline: Tata Power Solar Initiates Rooftop Campaign Across Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: Tata Power Solar has launched the 'Ghar Ghar Solar' campaign in Uttar Pradesh to accelerate the adoption of rooftop solar energy. In collaboration with Tata Power Battery Storage, the initiative aims to provide integrated green energy solutions and ensure reliable clean power across the state.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Dismisses Pernod Ricard Plea for Liquor License
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court rejected a petition by French spirits giant Pernod Ricard challenging the city government's refusal to grant a wholesale liquor license. The court ruled that the company is currently ineligible due to pending criminal proceedings under the state's excise regulations.

articles.headline: Asian Paints Reports 69.3 Percent Surge in Fourth Quarter Profit
articles.summary: Asian Paints reported a consolidated net profit of ₹1,172.1 crore for Q4 FY26, a growth of 69.3% year-on-year. Net sales rose 10.6% to ₹9,246.7 crore, driven by double-digit volume growth in the domestic decorative business and improved operational margins.

articles.headline: K2 Infragen Reports 14 Percent Profit Growth for FY26
articles.summary: Infrastructure firm K2 Infragen recorded a net profit of ₹13.32 crore for the 2025-26 fiscal year, up from ₹11.64 crore. Total income grew 27.19% to ₹188.92 crore. The company attributed the growth to a strong order book in energy, railways, and road sectors.

articles.headline: OpenAI Reports 27-Fold Increase in Weekly Active Users in India
articles.summary: Thomas Jung of OpenAI stated that weekly active users of its AI tools in India have grown 27 times since the start of 2026. He emphasized that AI should be viewed as a productivity enhancer for developers rather than a replacement for human talent, citing strong adoption in non-coding workflows.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Upholds 12-Minute Hourly Advertisement Limit for TV
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court upheld TRAI regulations limiting television advertisements to 12 minutes per hour. The court dismissed petitions from broadcasters, stating that the regulatory framework balances commercial interests with the public's right to a quality viewing experience without excessive interruptions.

articles.headline: Growth of Unique Mutual Fund Investors Slows Amid Market Volatility
articles.summary: New investor additions to the mutual fund industry fell 37% in April compared to March, reaching a multi-year low of 2.95 lakh. Analysts attribute the slowdown to market uncertainty, geopolitical tensions, and fewer new fund offers, leading retail investors to adopt a cautious approach.

articles.headline: Global Markets Remain Soft in May Due to Iran Crisis
articles.summary: Major global indices closed May with losses as uncertainty over US-Iran developments weighed on investor sentiment. India's Sensex dropped 2.8% during the month. In contrast, South Korea emerged as the top-performing market with a 28% gain, while gold and silver saw mixed trends.

articles.headline: Cash Remains Preferred Payment Method for Small Retailers and Households
articles.summary: An RBI survey shows that despite the rapid growth of digital payments, cash remains the preferred mode for households and small retailers. The value of currency in circulation increased by 11.9% to reach ₹41.23 lakh crore by March 2026, even as digital transaction volumes rose by 26.9%.

articles.headline: RBI to Conduct Cross-Border CBDC Pilots with Five Central Banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India plans to expand its Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilots in FY27 to include cross-border transactions. The RBI is collaborating with central banks in Germany, France, Japan, and Canada to test CBDC for wholesale and retail payment settlements.

articles.headline: India Targets 150 Billion Dollar Semiconductor Value Chain by 2035
articles.summary: A NITI Aayog report suggests India needs to build a $120-$150 billion semiconductor value chain by 2035 to reduce import dependency. The plan requires up to $180 billion in investment. The government is urged to commit one-third of the costs to attract global private capital.

articles.headline: Fertilizer Sales Jump as Farmers Increase Stocking Amid Geopolitical Tensions
articles.summary: Urea sales in India rose 10% and DAP sales surged 39% as farmers engaged in panic buying and stocking ahead of the Kharif season. The surge follows concerns over import prices due to the West Asia conflict. Government officials clarified that current stocks of 200 lakh tons are sufficient.

articles.headline: Government Urges Public Sector Banks to Prepare for Global Volatility
articles.summary: Financial Services Secretary M. Nagaraju advised public sector banks to maintain readiness and adapt strategies in response to evolving global crises. He noted that PSU banks recorded historic annual net profits of ₹1.98 lakh crore as of March 31, 2026.

articles.headline: Rising Water Scarcity and Heatwaves Pose Major Economic Challenges
articles.summary: Severe heatwaves and falling reservoir levels have triggered a water crisis across India, with 11 out of 15 major river basins facing stress. The editorial warns that current consumption patterns in sectors like ethanol and data centers are unsustainable given the rapid depletion of groundwater.

articles.headline: India Needs New Institutional Framework to Fund Long-Term Infrastructure
articles.summary: Former Union Minister Jayant Sinha argues that India must increase investment to 40% of GDP to fund energy transitions. He proposes a National Infrastructure Institution to manage domestic savings and attract global pension funds, aiming for consistent 15-18% returns in rupee terms.

articles.headline: India Increases Crude Oil Imports from Russia and Gas from US
articles.summary: India's crude oil imports from Russia rose to 19 lakh barrels per day in May, accounting for 38.6% of total imports. To ensure energy security amid West Asia tensions, the government has also boosted gas imports from the US and directed oil companies to maintain a 30-day LPG stock.

articles.headline: NPCI Proposes Regulatory Framework for AI Integration in Fintech
articles.summary: NPCI MD Dilip Asbe called for a formal regulatory framework to govern 'Agentic AI' in digital payments. He emphasized the need for governance standards to verify automated transactions and handle grievances, ensuring that AI-driven solutions benefit both consumers and merchants safely.

articles.headline: Mining Policy Should Focus on Production Over Revenue Generation
articles.summary: Vedanta founder Anil Agarwal emphasized that India's mining policy should prioritize output for commodities like oil and iron ore over maximizing auction premiums. He advocated for long-term leases and self-certification to encourage large-scale private investment and achieve self-reliance.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs High Courts to Deliver Judgments Within Three Months
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued a directive to all High Courts to deliver reserved judgments within a three-month deadline. If a verdict is delayed beyond this period, the Registrar General must report the matter to the Chief Justice to ensure accountability and prevent loss to litigants.

articles.headline: Ahmedabad Industry Braces for Profit Surge Ahead of IPL Final
articles.summary: Travel and hospitality sectors are seeing a massive surge in demand for the IPL final in Ahmedabad. Hotel rates have jumped up to 40% and flight prices have tripled as fans travel to see the Royal Challengers Bengaluru defend their title at the Narendra Modi Stadium.

articles.headline: Government Proposes Driver Index to Rate Commercial Vehicle Operators
articles.summary: The Ministry of Road Transport is developing a 'Driver Index' to rate commercial vehicle drivers based on their skills and safety records. The system will use data on past violations and license history to address the shortage of skilled drivers and improve efficiency in the logistics sector.

articles.headline: IndiGo Reports ₹2,536 Crore Q4 Loss Over Fuel and Labour Costs
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation reported a net loss of ₹2,536 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2026. The loss is attributed to rising fuel prices and a one-time ₹2,000-crore impact from implementing new labour codes. Despite the loss, revenue from operations rose by 18.3% to ₹9,228.5 crore compared to the previous year, though domestic demand remained inelastic to price hikes.

articles.headline: Anthropic Raises $6.5 Billion Funding, Valuation Nears $1 Trillion
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic has raised $6.5 billion in a new funding round led by Altimeter Capital, valuing the company at nearly $965 billion. The round included participation from Google, Amazon, and Samsung. Anthropic's annual revenue run rate is projected to exceed $50 billion by next month, significantly challenging OpenAI's market dominance in the generative AI sector.

articles.headline: MSCI Index Rejig and US-Iran Deal Worries Spook Indian Markets
articles.summary: Indian equity indices fell nearly 1.5% on Friday due to a combination of MSCI index rebalancing and global jitters over a potential US-Iran peace deal. The NSE Nifty dropped 359 points to close at 23,547.75, while the BSE Sensex fell 1.4% to 74,775.74. Foreign outflows were estimated at ₹8,000-8,500 crore during the final 30 minutes of trade as weightage triggers hit selling levels.

articles.headline: India’s Gold Demand Drops 70% Following Steep Import Duty Hike
articles.summary: Gold demand in India has plummeted by approximately 70% since the government increased the import duty to 15% from 6% effective May 13. High fuel and food prices have further dampened consumer sentiment. Industry bodies report that the unorganised trade, which comprises 65% of the gold market, has been hardest hit by the sudden price surge and tax increase during the Adhik Maas period.

articles.headline: RBI Lowers India’s FY27 GDP Growth Forecast to 6.9 Percent
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has revised India's real GDP growth forecast for 2026-27 downward to 6.9%, compared to the previous estimate of 7.6%. The central bank cited West Asia conflict-related supply chain disruptions and volatile global commodity prices as primary risks. However, the RBI noted that healthy corporate and bank balance sheets provide a positive macro outlook despite the risks.

articles.headline: Centre Plans Tax Incentives and Sops for Local Battery Manufacturing
articles.summary: The Indian government is discussing a new tax and fiscal incentive framework to localise the production of critical components for lithium-ion batteries. The plan aims to reduce reliance on Chinese imports, which account for 70% of the value of Li-ion cells. Incentives may be linked to output, targeting a capacity of 400k tonnes of cathode material by 2030 to secure the EV supply chain.

articles.headline: IMD Forecasts Below Normal Monsoon Rains for India This Year
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department has forecast that the 2026 southwest monsoon is likely to be below normal at 90% of the long-period average. The forecast is attributed to the building of El Niño conditions. Central India and the southern peninsula are expected to receive less than 94% of the LPA, potentially impacting agricultural output and food inflation across the country.

articles.headline: Taliban and Russia Ink Maiden Defence Pact to Combat Terror Threats
articles.summary: The Taliban-led Afghan government has signed its first military-technical cooperation agreement with Russia. The pact, signed in Moscow, includes exchange of intelligence and technology to counter security threats, particularly from groups like ISIS. While Russia has not officially recognised the Taliban, it recently removed the group from its list of terrorist organisations to foster ties.

articles.headline: OpenAI to Increase India Investments as Codex Tool Sees High Demand
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to significantly ramp up investments in India by early 2026, identifying the country as a top five global market for its artificial intelligence tools. Usage of Codex, an AI tool for developers, has grown 27 times since its launch in India. The company is partnering with Indian firms like TCS and Infosys to deploy generative AI solutions across the country's tech sector.

articles.headline: Intel and Odisha Sign $3.3 Billion Chip Substrate Manufacturing Deal
articles.summary: Intel has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Odisha government and 3D Glass Solutions to establish a $3.3 billion semiconductor substrate manufacturing facility. Located in Bhubaneswar, the project is expected to create over 1,800 high-skilled jobs. The facility will focus on advanced glass core substrates, a critical component for next-generation AI and high-speed computing.

articles.headline: Asian Paints Net Profit Jumps 70% Weighing ‘Calibrated’ Price Hikes
articles.summary: Asian Paints reported a 69% increase in net profit to ₹1,272.1 crore for the March quarter. The growth was driven by a 10% increase in domestic decorative volume and calibrated price hikes to offset rising input costs due to West Asia tensions. The company's board has recommended a final dividend of ₹23 per share for the fiscal year 2025-26, reflecting strong cash flow and market position.

articles.headline: India and Canada Launch Trade and Investment Forum to Boost Ties
articles.summary: India and Canada have formally launched a Trade and Investment Forum to enhance economic cooperation and explore a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement. Union Minister Piyush Goyal met with Canadian officials in New York to discuss strengthening supply chains in sectors like energy, mining, and technology. Both nations aim to conclude the trade agreement talks by the end of 2026.

articles.headline: PSG Faces Arsenal in High-Stakes Champions League Final in Budapest
articles.summary: Paris Saint-Germain will take on Arsenal in the Champions League final at Budapest's Puskas Arena. The match pits PSG’s prolific attack, led by Ousmane Dembélé, against Arsenal’s league-best defence. PSG seeks its first title, while Arsenal aims to win the trophy for the first time in its history. Both teams enter the final after dominant domestic league performances and high-scoring seasons.

articles.headline: New ‘Molecular Glue’ Drugs Show Promise in Treating Pancreatic Cancer
articles.summary: Scientists at Harvard have developed molecular glue drugs that can target previously undruggable proteins linked to pancreatic cancer. These drugs work by chemically gluing harmful proteins to the cell’s natural waste disposal system. Early clinical trials show significant improvements in survival rates, attracting billions in investment from major pharmaceutical companies like Eli Lilly.

articles.headline: Rupee Closes at 95 Against Dollar on Best Day Since April
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened to 94.95 against the US dollar on Friday, marking its best single-day performance since early April. The gains were supported by a pause in central bank dollar buying and positive sentiment following news of a potential peace deal between Iran and the US. Traders noted that lower crude prices also provided critical support to the domestic currency valuation.

articles.headline: US FDA Flags Dabur Plant Over Manufacturing Lapses at Silvassa
articles.summary: The US Food and Drug Administration has issued a warning to Dabur India regarding manufacturing and maintenance lapses at its plant in Silvassa. The regulator's report highlighted risks of microbiological contamination in health-care brands like Honitus. Dabur has stated it has already implemented corrective actions and is working with the FDA to resolve the compliance issues.

articles.headline: Tesla Lowers Model Y Prices by ₹9 Lakh Amid Competition
articles.summary: Tesla has reduced the price of its Model Y SUV in India by ₹9 lakh as it faces stiff competition from established luxury rivals and local EV makers. The price cut aims to stimulate demand in a slowing global EV market. The move follows similar price reductions by the company in China and the US as it seeks to maintain its market share against rising domestic competition.

articles.headline: RBI: India's FY27 growth positive, but headwinds persist
articles.summary: RBI's Annual Report for FY26 projects positive FY27 growth (6.9% real GDP) driven by strong macroeconomic fundamentals and domestic demand. However, geopolitical tensions (West Asia conflict), elevated energy prices, and supply chain disruptions pose downside risks to growth and inflation (projected at 4.6%). Banking system remains resilient.

articles.headline: US Ambassador Gor optimistic on India-US trade deal finalization
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor expressed optimism that an interim India-US trade deal could be finalised in "coming weeks and months," citing India's immense growth potential. He noted the Trump administration is recalibrating export controls and seeing India as a new centre of power. India, however, is cautious, awaiting US tariff situation developments.

articles.headline: IMD forecasts India's driest monsoon since 2015
articles.summary: The IMD scaled down its South-West monsoon projection to 90% of Long-Period Average (below-normal rainfall) for 2026, marking the driest year since 2015. Onset may be delayed to June 4-5, with potential loss of momentum later. El Nino emergence is a main reason. Core rainfed agriculture areas of Central India are likely to be below normal.

articles.headline: MCA allows ZCZP bonds for CSR, boosting Social Stock Exchange
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs allowed companies to channel up to 10% of their annual CSR spending through 'Zero Coupon Zero Principal' (ZCZP) instruments on the Social Stock Exchange (SSE). This aims to deepen India's SSE ecosystem, providing a philanthropic funding mechanism via a regulated market framework for Not-for-Profit Organisations.

articles.headline: Elgi Equipments positive on FY27 despite global shocks
articles.summary: Jairam Varadaraj, MD of Elgi Equipments, expects double-digit growth in FY26 and remains positive for FY27, citing strong customer demand. He acknowledged rising input costs, particularly due to the West Asia crisis, but noted cost reductions and calibrated price hikes helped profitability. He also flagged Chinese dumping as a major concern.

articles.headline: Infrastructure project additions surge 12-fold in Q4 FY26
articles.summary: India's newly-added infrastructure projects jumped 12-fold in Q4 FY26 to 483, driven mainly by road and highway projects, according to MoSPI data. Project costs rose 544.9% year-on-year to ₹6.01 lakh crore. Roads and highways accounted for 439 projects worth ₹4.13 lakh crore, or 69% of total project costs.

articles.headline: Inox Wind Q4 profit drops 44% due to execution delays
articles.summary: Inox Wind Ltd reported a 44% year-on-year drop in consolidated net profit to ₹106 crore in Q4 FY26, with revenue declining 2.4%. Execution delays, supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions, and delays in critical equipment impacted performance. Full-year profit, however, rose 3% to ₹449 crore.

articles.headline: MM Forgings' domestic sales boost Q4 profit amid global slowdown
articles.summary: Chennai-based MM Forgings Ltd saw stronger domestic market traction in Q4 FY26, contributing 71% of revenue (up from 63%). Consolidated net profit rose 36% to ₹45 crore. Demand from key export markets like the US and Europe softened due to global economic uncertainties and a slow truck segment in the US.

articles.headline: Asian Paints Q4 profit up 69% on better realisations
articles.summary: Asian Paints reported a 69% increase in net profit to ₹1,185 crore in the March quarter, with revenue up 11% to ₹9,247 crore. Better realisations, cost discipline, material deflation, and operational efficiencies boosted margins. Domestic decorative business volume grew 12%, and industrial business saw strong double-digit growth.

articles.headline: Natco Pharma's Q4 consolidated net profit declines 34%
articles.summary: Natco Pharma’s consolidated net profit declined 34% at ₹269 crore in Q4 FY26 compared to ₹406 crore last year. Total revenue also decreased 36.5% at ₹816 crore against ₹1,287 crore in the year-ago period.

articles.headline: GACL to build high-purity hydrogen peroxide plant for semicon, solar
articles.summary: Gujarat Alkalies and Chemicals Ltd (GACL) will invest ₹67 crore to set up a 5,000 TPA high-purity hydrogen peroxide plant at Dahej, Gujarat. This facility targets rising demand from semiconductor fabrication units and solar cell manufacturers, supporting India's electronics and clean energy ecosystems. The project aims for ₹42 crore annual revenue.

articles.headline: Toyota Urban Cruiser Ebella EV priced ₹23.60 lakh, delayed launch
articles.summary: Toyota Kirloskar Motor (TKM) priced its Urban Cruiser Ebella electric SUV at ₹23.60 lakh (ex-showroom) for the E3 variant. The launch, delayed by over four months, enters India's crowded EV market. The Ebella, sharing its platform with Maruti Suzuki e Vitara, uses a 61 kWh battery and 128 kW motor. Deliveries start mid-June.

articles.headline: India's forex reserves fall $7.511 billion to $681.384 billion
articles.summary: India’s forex reserves dropped by $7.511 billion to $681.384 billion during the week ended May 22, the RBI reported. This follows an $8.094 billion drop in the previous reporting week. Foreign currency assets decreased by $2.872 billion.

articles.headline: India's services exports up 12.7% in April
articles.summary: India’s services exports grew by 12.7% to $37.021 billion in April, registering the highest growth in the calendar year. Imports also expanded 8.9% to $18.417 billion, according to RBI data.

articles.headline: India, Canada advance CEPA talks amid 'two-track' approach
articles.summary: India and Canada are advancing bilateral free trade agreement negotiations, targeting a year-end completion. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and Canadian counterpart Maninder Sidhu reaffirmed this. Canada is adopting a "two-track" approach, boosting economic dialogue while pursuing public safety cooperation on law enforcement. A 'team Canada' trade mission will visit India later this year.

articles.headline: CEA: Energy prices, CAD, BoP top priorities amid West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran stated that managing macroeconomic fundamentals like balance of payments (BoP) and current account deficit (CAD) has become a larger priority due to the "energy shock" from the West Asia conflict. He also commented on income limits for microfinance and priority sector lending.

articles.headline: Industrial users boost petrol, diesel sales in 20% of districts
articles.summary: Nearly 20% of India's districts are seeing unusually high petrol and diesel sales, partly due to industrial users buying cheaper fuel from retail outlets meant for consumers. The Oil Ministry noted adequate stocks but highlighted ethical concerns. Enforcement actions, including raids and arrests, have been initiated against malpractice.

articles.headline: India's LPG production hits record 52,000 tonnes/day
articles.summary: India's LPG production reached a record 52,000 tonnes per day (TPD) following refinery resumptions, up from 46,000 TPD earlier this month. Current demand is around 72,000 TPD. LPG supply remains affected by the West Asia conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure, impacting 60% of India's demand, mostly from the Middle East.

articles.headline: Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 Apex: A focused and complete offering
articles.summary: The new Royal Enfield Guerrilla 450 Apex variant (₹2.49 lakh ex-showroom) refines the original with a fetching red shade, fairing, and seat cowl, along with sporty Vredestein Centauro ST+ tyres. Powered by a 452 cc Sherpa engine (39.45 bhp), it offers strong mid-range torque. Despite some minor niggles, it's a more focused and trustworthy motorcycle.

articles.headline: Mercedes-Benz CLA 250+ EV reviewed: Premium appeal, firm ride
articles.summary: The Mercedes-Benz CLA 250+, Mercedes' newest EV in India, is priced at ₹59 lakh (ex-showroom). The AMG Line spec model features a compact design with EV elements and a sporty profile. It offers a 792 km WLTP range (over 500 km real-world) from an 85-kWh battery and 268 bhp motor. However, firm ride quality and screen-dependency are noted.

articles.headline: Hero Super Splendor XTEC 2.0 launched at ₹86,500
articles.summary: Hero MotoCorp launched the Super Splendor XTEC 2.0 at ₹86,500. This 125cc commuter motorcycle features updated styling, tech additions like Bluetooth-enabled digital instrumentation and USB Type-C charging, and claims a fuel efficiency of 72 kpl from its 124.7cc engine (10.7 bhp).

articles.headline: Triumph Street Triple 765 RX debuts at ₹13.91 lakh
articles.summary: Triumph's track-focused Street Triple 765 RX has debuted in India at ₹13.91 lakh. It retains the 765cc inline-triple engine (128.2 bhp) but adds fully adjustable Öhlins NIX30 front forks, clip-on handlebars, a dedicated performance seat, and exclusive RX styling. It's available only through 2026.

articles.headline: JSW MG Motor India launches Majestor SUV at ₹41 lakh
articles.summary: JSW MG Motor India launched its flagship ICE SUV, the Majestor, priced at ₹41 lakh. It features a 2.0-litre twin-turbo diesel engine (215 bhp), 8-speed automatic gearbox, 4x2/4x4 options, in-your-face styling, triple differential locks, multiple terrain modes, and a feature-rich cabin with ADAS and panoramic sunroof.

articles.headline: RBI to prioritise customer protection, credit ecosystem in FY27
articles.summary: The RBI's regulatory agenda for FY27 focuses on customer protection and strengthening the credit ecosystem. It aims to enhance supervisory practices, including reviewing KYC/AML, strengthening data-driven supervision, and examining shared loan arrangements. It plans to expand CBDC pilots, scale up the unified lending interface, and extend video-based KYC to NRI customers.

articles.headline: RBI balance sheet grows 21% in FY26 on gold valuation, investments
articles.summary: The RBI’s balance sheet expanded 20.6% to ₹91.97 lakh crore in FY26, driven by a 63.8% surge in gold holdings' value and a 44.9% rise in domestic investments (to ₹22.59 lakh crore). Higher interest earnings from securities and foreign exchange gains boosted income. The contingency fund increased to ₹5.68 lakh crore.

articles.headline: Domestic bond yields face upward pressure from global shocks: RBI
articles.summary: The RBI's annual report warned that domestic bond yields could face upward pressure if global monetary easing stalls due to persistent oil price shocks and West Asia fragility. However, government fiscal consolidation and RBI liquidity measures are expected to contain this. G-sec yields hardened in Q4 FY26 due to higher borrowings and crude prices.

articles.headline: Bank time deposits, debt MFs are complementary investments: RBI
articles.summary: An RBI analysis indicates that bank time deposits and debt mutual funds act as complementary, not competing, investment avenues in India. This structural segmentation minimizes direct competition, fostering concurrent allocation of domestic savings. Equity mutual funds also saw significant AUM growth (32.4%) in FY26.

articles.headline: Rupee increasingly used for import, export invoicing: RBI
articles.summary: The RBI reported increasing use of the rupee for import and export invoicing, benefiting trading partners by offering exchange rate risk protection and reducing transaction costs. Rupee-based invoicing and settlement have picked up significantly since July 2022, with FY26 seeing 6.5% growth in export invoicing and 9.5% in import invoicing.

articles.headline: ₹500 note circulation up 11%, fake notes detected jump 20%
articles.summary: The volume of ₹500 banknotes in circulation rose 11.2% in FY26, reinforcing their dominance (41.2% of total banknotes). In value, they formed over 86% of currency in circulation. However, detected counterfeit ₹500 notes increased over 20%. Overall banknote volume and value increased 10.5% and 11.9% respectively.

articles.headline: Financial entities report 10,114 fraud cases worth ₹48,021 crore in FY26
articles.summary: Banks and financial institutions reported 10,114 fraud cases totaling ₹48,021 crore in FY26, compared to 23,722 cases worth ₹32,803 crore in FY25. While fraud numbers decreased, the amount involved increased. Advances category accounted for the largest share in value. Digital payment fraud cases saw a significant decline in numbers.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank denies material impact of ₹45 crore payment issue
articles.summary: HDFC Bank denied a media report alleging a ₹45 crore payment issue involving disguising interest payments as marketing expenditure for a Maharashtra government entity. The bank stated its internal controls are robust, the matter has no material impact on financial statements, and internal audit observations are addressed. Regulatory scrutiny is possible.

articles.headline: Karnataka leadership change: A high stakes gamble for Congress
articles.summary: The Congress's replacement of Siddaramaiah with DK Shivakumar as Karnataka CM is seen as a high-stakes gamble. It risks alienating Siddaramaiah's AHINDA base and shifts focus from pro-poor welfarism to urban infrastructure, which faces public unease. Shivakumar will need to carefully balance caste equations and fiscal challenges in the fiscally stretched state.

articles.headline: West Asia crisis intensifies cost, supply risks for Indian industries
articles.summary: Three months into the West Asia conflict, energy-intensive Indian industries (aviation, refining, chemicals, fertilizers) face pronounced challenges from elevated input costs and operational disruptions. Consumption and export-oriented sectors like cement and auto components also experience moderate headwinds. Banking sector profitability and credit growth may be impacted by rising economic stress.

articles.headline: Generali Central Life declares ₹102 crore FY26 bonus
articles.summary: Generali Central Life Insurance announced a bonus of ₹102 crore for FY26, a 21% growth from last year. This declaration will benefit over 95,000 policyholders, reinforcing the company’s commitment to delivering sustained value to policyholders.

articles.headline: Moody’s upgrades Tata Steel rating to Baa2
articles.summary: Moody’s Ratings upgraded Tata Steel's issuer risk rating to Baa2 from Baa3. The stable outlook reflects expectation of extraordinary support from its parent Tata Sons in a stress scenario and that earnings will strengthen as growth plans are executed.

articles.headline: India needs $180 billion for competitive semiconductor ecosystem: NITI Aayog
articles.summary: A NITI Aayog report states India requires $135–180 billion in semiconductor investment over the next decade to build a competitive ecosystem. The government should commit at least one-third to de-risk projects. Strategic partnerships are crucial for design, manufacturing, packaging, materials, and talent. India's semiconductor demand is projected to reach $90 billion by FY2030.

articles.headline: Maharashtra to provide 2,000 GPUs for AI innovators
articles.summary: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis announced the state will provide access to 2,000 GPUs for innovators to develop and train AI models, creating a "compute-as-a-service" facility. This initiative is part of Maharashtra's AI policy, aiming for ₹10,000 crore investment in AI and 125 lakh new jobs.

articles.headline: IIFCL equity dilution gets government nod
articles.summary: The government has approved equity dilution in India Infrastructure Finance Company Ltd (IIFCL) to bring government's equity to 75%. MD Rohit Rishi stated IPO timing is not finalized but will be opportune. IIFCL sanctioned a record ₹57,680 crore in FY26, though net profit declined to ₹1,379 crore due to foreign exchange loss.

articles.headline: Pipavav Port cargo volumes to fall due to West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Gujarat Pipavav Port Ltd expects a sharp shift in Q1 cargo mix due to the West Asia conflict. Liquid cargo volumes are projected to fall 35-40%, and bulk cargo (fertilizers, LPG) 8-10% sequentially. Despite disruptions, container volumes are expected to grow 5-7% with new shipping routes, including Maersk's FI2 service.

articles.headline: AVI Polymers board to consider stock-split, bonus issue
articles.summary: AVI Polymers' board will meet to consider a stock-split (up to 1:10) and a bonus issue (up to 10:1) to enhance shareholder value and liquidity. The company is also considering entry into green technology and sustainability-focused business segments.

articles.headline: Sensex crashes 1,000 points on MSCI rejig, foreign outflows
articles.summary: Indian markets closed sharply lower, with Sensex declining 1,092 points and Nifty 359 points, due to MSCI's May 2026 index rebalancing. This triggered an estimated $800 million to $1 billion in passive foreign outflows. Heavy selling in Reliance Industries and banking majors deepened the decline. IMD's monsoon forecast also rattled sentiment.

articles.headline: SEBI simplifies nomination process for demat accounts, MFs
articles.summary: SEBI eased nomination norms for demat accounts and mutual fund folios, making it a default option for new single-holder accounts from September 1. It also relaxed procedural requirements for physical nominations and reduced mandatory information. Investors can nominate up to three individuals, with equal division if no share is specified.

articles.headline: SEBI approves Upma Chawdhry as MSE Chairperson
articles.summary: The Metropolitan Stock Exchange of India (MSE) announced SEBI's approval of Upma Dada Chawdhry as the Chairperson of its governing board. Chawdhry, a 1983-batch Indian Administrative Service officer, brings over 35 years of administrative and governance expertise to the role.

articles.headline: Motilal Oswal neutral on equities, sees mid-/small-cap opportunities
articles.summary: Motilal Oswal Private Wealth maintains a neutral outlook on Indian equities due to geopolitical uncertainty, crude prices, and slowing earnings. However, they see opportunities in mid- and small-cap segments due to strong earnings growth (around 30% Y-o-Y in Q4). They recommend a staggered approach for pure equity strategies and favor gold.

articles.headline: Mid-, small-cap earnings growth positive for FY27 outlook
articles.summary: Ankit Patel of Arunasset Investment Services believes strong earnings growth in mid- and small-cap markets keeps the broader market outlook positive for FY27, despite recent consolidation. DII participation and aggressive buying during corrections have been key drivers of market resilience. However, a weak monsoon could impact rural consumption.

articles.headline: IndiGo addresses forex exposure, fleet strategy for FY27
articles.summary: IndiGo's parent, InterGlobe Aviation, faces significant foreign exchange exposure (nearly $10 billion, $1.3 billion hedged) and forex volatility remains a key challenge. The airline plans mid-teen unit revenue growth in Q1 FY27 and expects grounded aircraft numbers to reduce. It aims to acquire aircraft outright to cut lease costs and lower forex exposure.

articles.headline: Happiest Minds projects 12.5% revenue growth for FY27
articles.summary: Happiest Minds reported Q4 FY26 total income of ₹621.7 crore (up 9% Y-o-Y) and PAT of ₹61.2 crore (up 79.9% Y-o-Y). For FY26, revenue grew 12.3% to ₹2,315.1 crore. The company is confident of achieving 12.5% revenue growth in FY27, citing embedded AI across offerings and strong demand, particularly from the GCC region.

articles.headline: 63SATS Cybertech expects high demand for AI-aided cyber defenses
articles.summary: 63SATS Cybertech, 63 moons technologies' cybersecurity arm, anticipates a surge in demand for AI-powered cyber defenses and DPDP Act compliance services. With the DPDP Act rollout in May 2027, the company is focusing on end-to-end privacy architecture and AI CyberOps models to address rapid threat timelines and 'shadow AI' internal data leaks.

articles.headline: Skoda Auto Volkswagen expands to Sri Lanka with new brand launch
articles.summary: Skoda Auto Volkswagen India Private Ltd (SAVWIPL) launched the Volkswagen brand in Sri Lanka via local partner Continental Cars and Commercials Ltd, opening a 3S facility in Colombo. This expansion is part of SAVWIPL's export-led strategy, with initial models including the Taigun SUV and Virtus sedan, manufactured at its Pune plant.

articles.headline: Gold gains on US-Iran ceasefire optimism, but weekly decline likely
articles.summary: Spot gold rose for a second session, up 0.6% to $4,519.64/ounce, following news of a potential US-Iran ceasefire extension. However, prices are still headed for a weekly decline due to inflation concerns and expectations of higher interest rates.

articles.headline: Fertilizer sufficient for kharif, but rabi season concerns: Secy
articles.summary: India has sufficient fertilizers for the kharif season but may face problems during the rabi season due to current geopolitical tensions, according to Fertilizer Secretary Rajat Kumar Mishra. He noted excessive use and industrial diversion of fertilizers. Urea sales surged 10%, and gas procurement costs are 70-80% higher due to the war.

articles.headline: India's major dams' storage drops to 30% capacity
articles.summary: Storage in India's 166 major reservoirs fell to 30.67% of capacity (56.3 BCM) this week, lower than the year-ago and normal levels, according to CWC data. 29% of districts experienced deficient rainfall. Storage in the east and south dropped below 25%, raising concerns amid a potential below-normal South-West monsoon.

articles.headline: Union Agri Minister Chouhan urges states to prepare for below-normal monsoon
articles.summary: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan urged states to prepare for deficient rainfall and adverse weather, citing IMD's "below-normal" monsoon forecast (90% of LPA) amid El Niño threat. He emphasized contingency plans, adequate seed/fertilizer availability, and balanced fertilizer use. Discussions with banks will enhance agricultural credit access.

articles.headline: Government unveils AI-driven roadmap for fisheries sector
articles.summary: The Centre launched a roadmap integrating AI, satellite communication, and digital traceability to monitor India's fisheries sector. AI is key to combating illegal fishing, enhancing fisher safety, and building transparent supply chains. Nationwide expansion of the ReALCraft digital platform for fishing vessel registration is a major highlight, with over 2.25 lakh vessels already registered.

articles.headline: CCI resumes cotton sale, cuts prices by ₹2,300/candy
articles.summary: The Cotton Corporation of India (CCI) resumed cotton sales from the 2025-26 season, cutting prices by ₹2,300 per candy (356 kg) due to easing global prices. However, trade response was muted due to price mismatch and weak yarn prices. CCI has procured about 105 lakh bales, with around 32 lakh bales remaining in stock.

articles.headline: Natural diamond ownership rising among Indian women: De Beers
articles.summary: A De Beers India Diamond Acquisition Study 2025 by the Natural Diamonds Council (NDC) found that natural diamond jewellery ownership among Indian women increased by 4 percentage points to 15% in 2025. This rise is driven by younger consumers (Gen Z and Millennials) and reflects growing aspiration and accessibility, with the market projected to grow at 12% CAGR.

articles.headline: Kerala to launch Women Farmers Consortium, boost agri sector
articles.summary: Kerala Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar announced a comprehensive plan to identify and integrate women farmers across the agricultural value chain through a Women Farmers Consortium. Special schemes will support paddy, vegetable, fruit farming, and beekeeping. The initiative aims to improve farm incomes, strengthen procurement, and promote modern technology.

articles.headline: Golden Tips Tea plans expansion in West Bengal
articles.summary: Golden Tips Tea, an old Indian tea brand, plans to enter the general trade across West Bengal within six months and aims for 100 franchise outlets pan-India in three years. The company currently has 15 own stores and 16 franchise stores in West Bengal and Sikkim and exports teas to China, Russia, and Japan.

articles.headline: Renewed West Asia conflict impacts Kochi tea auctions
articles.summary: Renewed West Asia tensions are impacting Kochi's tea auction market, making overseas buyers cautious. Weekly intake of orthodox teas has declined, leading to higher unsold quantities. Traders report payment delays from overseas buyers. CTC dust market is also expected to remain weak, though a slight improvement is anticipated towards end-June.

articles.headline: Kurve Sachin appointed Chennai Port Authority Chairperson
articles.summary: Kurve Sachin Shardchandra has taken charge as Chairperson of the Chennai Port Authority (ChPA). He succeeds Sunil Paliwal, who is now serving as Chairman of the Inland Waterways Authority of India.

articles.headline: PM Surya Ghar scheme solarizes 40 lakh households
articles.summary: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi announced that 40 lakh households have been solarized under the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. Launched in February 2024, the scheme aims to install rooftop solar systems in one crore households by March 2027, turning consumers into power producers.

articles.headline: Gig platforms must sync worker data with e-Shram by June 22
articles.summary: The Union Labour Ministry set a June 22 deadline for platform businesses to transfer all workers' data to the e-Shram portal. This move aims to operationalize the 'National Social Security Board for Gig and Platform Workers' under the Code on Social Security. Over one crore workers are employed in the sector.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM Shivakumar faces challenges balancing welfare, caste, infra
articles.summary: Newly appointed Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar faces challenges balancing welfare, caste, and infrastructure development. The state's finances are stretched by welfare guarantees (₹20,000 crore additional borrowing annually). He must also navigate the contentious caste census report and demands for cabinet representation, while addressing the state's per capita debt which surged to ₹1.12 lakh.

articles.headline: Kerala outlines industrial growth, investment reforms in Governor's address
articles.summary: Kerala Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar outlined the UDF government's vision to transform the state into a globally competitive, innovation-driven industrial economy. Key initiatives include port-led logistics, industrial parks, and sector-focused ecosystems. The government plans to formalize the Investment Promotion Board and modernize K-SWIFT for faster approvals, aiming to attract investments.

articles.headline: West Bengal halves e-way bill threshold to ₹50,000 for compliance
articles.summary: West Bengal's government reduced the threshold limit for intra-state e-way bills from ₹1 lakh to ₹50,000, effective June 1. This move aims to strengthen compliance and prevent tax evasion, potentially increasing state tax revenues. However, small traders are concerned about increased workload and compliance costs.

articles.headline: SHGs vital for sustainable farming, organic products: Karnataka Grameena Bank Chairman
articles.summary: Shreekant M Bhandiwad, Chairman of Karnataka Grameena Bank (KGB), highlighted the vital role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in conserving indigenous seeds, promoting natural farming, processing millets, and encouraging organic agricultural products. He praised Bibi Fatima Women SHG for establishing a community seed bank and millet processing unit.

articles.headline: Intel, Odisha, 3DGS sign deal for semicon substrate production
articles.summary: Intel Corporations, the Odisha government, and 3DGS signed an MoU for a semiconductor substrate manufacturing facility in Bhubaneswar-Khurda. The ₹3.3 billion project, Intel's first engagement in India for advanced packaging, aims to generate over 1,800 direct high-skilled jobs and contribute to India's semiconductor ecosystem.

articles.headline: Maersk to launch FI2 India-China ocean service
articles.summary: AP Moller–Maersk will launch a dedicated FI2 ocean service connecting Far East Asia to the Indian subcontinent, with the first sailing from Shanghai on June 4. The weekly service, using six vessels, will call at Pipavav, providing faster, high-frequency connectivity to key manufacturing and sourcing hubs, and rail connectivity to the NCR.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry launches common portal for unclaimed assets
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry launched a single unified online platform (unclaimedassetsportal.in) for citizens to search and trace unclaimed bank deposits, insurance claims, shares, and mutual funds. This initiative aims to enhance public awareness and ease access to information, contributing to financial empowerment.

articles.headline: SC's 28% retrospective GST ruling threatens real money gaming firms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the 28% retrospective GST levy on real-money online gaming platforms, applying it to the full face value of bets. This ruling revives tax demands of nearly ₹92,000 crore, far exceeding many companies' cash reserves and threatening insolvency. Firms are reassessing business models, with some shifting to ad-supported gaming or overseas expansion.

articles.headline: Airlines seek digital platform for Ebola self-declaration forms
articles.summary: Airlines have requested the government to roll out a digital platform, like the Air Suvidha app, for passenger self-declaration forms related to Ebola virus containment measures. This would improve compliance and save printing costs. The DGCA had asked airlines to distribute forms for flights from Uganda and DRC.

articles.headline: India sends first tranche of medical supplies to Africa for Ebola
articles.summary: India has sent its first tranche of medical supplies to the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) in response to the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda. The WHO declared the outbreak a public health emergency, and India is committed to further assistance.

articles.headline: Ebola treatments, Oxford/SII vaccine candidates under assessment
articles.summary: Advisory groups convened by WHO are assessing promising candidates for Ebola prevention and treatment, including the ChAdOx1 Bundibugyo vaccine developed by Oxford University/Serum Institute of India. This vaccine could be available for efficacy assessment in 2-3 months. Other therapeutics like monoclonal antibodies and oral antiviral obeldesivir are also being evaluated for clinical trials.

articles.headline: War and West Asia Conflict Threaten India Economic Stability RBI Warns
articles.summary: The RBI annual report warns that the West Asia conflict is the biggest threat to global and domestic stability. It raises concerns over inflation, energy prices, and trade disruptions. The RBI retained India's FY27 GDP growth estimate at 6.9% but noted rising risks. Consumer price inflation is projected at 4.6% as high energy prices and adverse weather intensify supply-side pressures.

articles.headline: IMD Lowers Monsoon Forecast to 90 Percent of Long Period Average
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) lowered its monsoon forecast to 90% of the 50-year average, squashing hopes for heatwave relief. Rains are expected to take another seven days to arrive. Below-normal rainfall is predicted for Central, South Peninsular, and Northwest India. This raises concerns over kharif sowing, reservoir levels, and rural consumption for fiscal year 2027.

articles.headline: Apple CFO Kevan Parekh Highlights Immense Investment Opportunities in India
articles.summary: Apple Senior VP and CFO Kevan Parekh, visiting India to meet developers, stated that the company expects significant growth in scale in the country. He noted that success is defined by impacting people on a broad scale via iOS innovation. Parekh highlighted that AI is lowering barriers for students and that India's App Store ecosystem facilitated nearly ₹44.5 thousand crore in 2024.

articles.headline: Supreme Court clears Reliance Industries in 2007 Petroleum fraud case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court exonerated Reliance Industries of fraudulent trading charges in the 2007 Reliance Petroleum futures case. Setting aside a Securities Appellate Tribunal order, the court directed the refund of ₹250 crore deposited by the company. However, it upheld a ₹25 crore penalty for disclosure violations. The bench ruled that SAT committed an 'egregious error' in finding fraud.

articles.headline: Bank Fraud Cases in India Rise 46% to ₹48,021 Crore
articles.summary: The RBI's annual report reveals that bank fraud cases in India increased by 46% to ₹48,021 crore in FY26, despite a drop in the total number of reported incidents. Digital payment frauds, particularly those involving cards and internet banking, accounted for the highest volume of cases. The report noted that private sector banks reported a higher number of frauds compared to public sector banks.

articles.headline: Tesla Launches Most Affordable Model Y Premium Variation in India
articles.summary: Tesla introduced its 2026 Model Y Premium rear-wheel drive in India, priced at ₹50.89 lakh. This is the company's most affordable offering in the country to date. It follows the launch of the Model YL, a three-row SUV priced at ₹61.99 lakh. The move is part of Tesla's strategy to expand its product portfolio and capture a larger share of the Indian luxury electric vehicle segment.

articles.headline: Supreme Court mandates bail rulings within 24 hours of hearing
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued binding directions to all high courts to decide on bail applications on the same day or within 24 hours of hearing. Invoking Article 142, the court also fixed a three-month limit for reserved judgments. Bail orders must be communicated to jail authorities on the day of pronouncement, and prisoners must be released the same day or the next day at the latest.

articles.headline: IndiGo reports unexpected Q4 loss of ₹2,536.9 crore
articles.summary: IndiGo, India's largest airline, posted a net loss of ₹2,536.9 crore for the March quarter. The airline cited high cost pressures, operational disruptions, rupee depreciation, and softer demand due to the West Asia conflict as primary reasons. Despite a 9.5% capacity expansion, load factors fell to 84.4%, and revenue per available seat kilometre dropped by 3%.

articles.headline: Amit Shah announces new 'territorial security' concept for BSF
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah revealed a plan to revamp the Border Security Force’s role by introducing 'territorial security.' The new framework involves sharing primary responsibility between the public, civil administration, local police, and the military. Shah stated the Centre is constructing a 'leak-proof security grid' with technological fencing along the Pakistan and Bengal borders.

articles.headline: Truck Driver Killed in Militant Firing on Manipur’s National Highway-202
articles.summary: A truck driver was killed and a police personnel injured during an ambush by suspected militants along National Highway-202 in Manipur's Ukhrul district. The incident occurred despite ongoing security operations to ensure the movement of essential goods. Chief Minister N. Biren Singh condemned the attack, as Naga and Kuki groups continue an economic blockade over the issue of missing persons.

articles.headline: Happiest Minds' Q4 Profit Climbs 80% to ₹61.17 Crore
articles.summary: IT firm Happiest Minds Technologies reported a 79.9% jump in net profit to ₹61.17 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2026. Revenue from operations grew by 10.9% to ₹501 crore. Executive Chairman Ashok Soota noted that the company is successfully navigating previous industry headwinds and is seeing strong growth in generative AI and cloud-native application platforms.

articles.headline: SEBI fines Suzlon Energy, executives for misleading finances
articles.summary: SEBI imposed penalties totalling ₹29 crore on Suzlon Energy and four former executives, including its Chairman and Vice-Chairman. The fine is for creating a misleading financial picture through intra-group transactions between FY14 and FY21, artificially inflating net worth and reducing reported losses, overturning an earlier exoneration.

articles.headline: RBI Earns ₹1.69 Lakh Crore Profit from Record Dollar Sales in FY26
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India generated a profit of ₹1.69 lakh crore from foreign exchange sales in the 2025-26 fiscal year, a 52% increase from the previous year. The gains were driven by heavy dollar selling to support the rupee amid global market volatility. Despite these interventions, India's total foreign exchange reserves fell by $7.5 billion to $681.3 billion as of May 22.

articles.headline: US Ambassador Says Indo-US Trade Pact Could Be Finalised in Weeks
articles.summary: US Ambassador Eric Garcetti stated that a major trade and investment agreement between India and the United States could be concluded within weeks. The deal aims to enhance cooperation in critical technologies, including semiconductors and space exploration. Garcetti highlighted that US-India trade has reached $205 billion, with the US now serving as India's largest trading partner.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Seeks NTA Accountability Over NEET-UG Paper Leak Claims
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concern over adhocism in the National Testing Agency's handling of the NEET-UG exam following paper leak allegations. The court asked NTA to file an affidavit on safeguards taken for the upcoming June 21 re-test. The bench emphasized that the NTA must ensure the process is foolproof to maintain the sanctity of national-level examinations for medical courses.

articles.headline: SC demands NTA accountability for NEET-UG paper leak
articles.summary: The Supreme Court criticised the National Testing Agency (NTA) for the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, noting it traumatised students. The court questioned NTA's repeated mistakes and demanded an affidavit from the Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) on how it plans to address the lack of "physical and intellectual wherewithal" and improve institutional capacity within NTA.
